            - [b2c7fcaf48cc3606980ef2ea61d86c58f09297f9](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/b2c7fcaf48cc3606980ef2ea61d86c58f09297f9.md) -- To use variable substitution, use the format ${<variable_name>} instead of defining a specific value.
            - [c74def7bf1b935dc5b93fc08e96b688099ec9ba9](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/c74def7bf1b935dc5b93fc08e96b688099ec9ba9.md) -- Test a SOAP message to validate the configuration before using the message in an integration.
            - [b71cb40649aaece54c03dbf93e8db1e8b7888e9a](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/b71cb40649aaece54c03dbf93e8db1e8b7888e9a.md) -- When creating SOAP message functions, you can configure the function to be sent through a MID Server.
            - [28fff74ea1872111349490ae74246a0395719ea4](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/28fff74ea1872111349490ae74246a0395719ea4.md) -- Create a SOAP message from a WSDL and external XSD file.
          - [917ba2c7d7c519f0ebefefc20b326dc4d38afdbe](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/917ba2c7d7c519f0ebefefc20b326dc4d38afdbe.md) -- For SOAP requests initiated from your ServiceNow instance to be able to successfully communicate with the web service provider inside a remote network, the ServiceNow instance must have HTTP or HTTPS access to the SOAP endpoint at the provider.
          - [f949752c074edd899d1fabbd895e57da35406a38](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/f949752c074edd899d1fabbd895e57da35406a38.md) -- You can authenticate outbound SOAP messages using several different security protocols.
            - [aa98b6380308f5b49034b6d0e01ec39840e9578c](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/aa98b6380308f5b49034b6d0e01ec39840e9578c.md) -- If the endpoint requires a user name and password, you can provide credentials using basic authentication.
            - [a4f7c2f8f275d285e4705d1530bde594e88e8dce](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/a4f7c2f8f275d285e4705d1530bde594e88e8dce.md) -- You can sign outbound SOAP messages using username and password or a key store and trusted server certificate saved on the instance.
          - [6bd6ec162d90cb5942d0ffd340fd1a56f0782778](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/6bd6ec162d90cb5942d0ffd340fd1a56f0782778.md) -- Certain properties provide support for SOAP requests to use a web proxy server.
          - [082d5779503115297a9ceaefa913b56ca03c5bf9](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/082d5779503115297a9ceaefa913b56ca03c5bf9.md) -- You can send outbound SOAP requests from any place in the ServiceNow AI Platform where scripting is allowed.
            - [1c6385ae4c8ac5e7231f467762ce4e65633fd2f5](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/1c6385ae4c8ac5e7231f467762ce4e65633fd2f5.md) -- Generate a sample script to send the SOAP message.
            - [6fafdb4b9c23ff200a36c6fa3ffc785f38d09b84](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/6fafdb4b9c23ff200a36c6fa3ffc785f38d09b84.md) -- You can send an outbound SOAP message directly to the endpoint.
            - [f1dc3c4db2e030761971d0f438bd5887849bd30b](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/f1dc3c4db2e030761971d0f438bd5887849bd30b.md) -- You can send an outbound SOAP message asynchronously.
            - [a114fabf847b25b1988b8e264bb1ea71635cb4b6](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/a114fabf847b25b1988b8e264bb1ea71635cb4b6.md) -- You can send an outbound SOAP message through a MID Server.
            - [25b62b82b93c613a844aeb57aeb7c993f0d1852d](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/25b62b82b93c613a844aeb57aeb7c993f0d1852d.md) -- You can use the SOAPMessageV2() constructor with no parameters to define a SOAP message entirely in the script.
        - [cfb415cab24da3beb5464fe597106c91bb688458](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/cfb415cab24da3beb5464fe597106c91bb688458.md) -- Log requests and responses for outbound web services such as REST and SOAP.
          - [d962831473abf00e3800ca0d5ff1d6dbc5fd99d0](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/d962831473abf00e3800ca0d5ff1d6dbc5fd99d0.md) -- You can configure outbound request logging to log basic, elevated, or all HTTP request and response information for specific domains.
            - [4b1e5062d62f8fda0fd3cae7c50d999f6847d10a](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/4b1e5062d62f8fda0fd3cae7c50d999f6847d10a.md) -- Certain elements are logged based on the configured log level.
          - [5661417c7c83a4786deb652a545f730e8d745fad](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/5661417c7c83a4786deb652a545f730e8d745fad.md) -- When you exclude outbound request logging for a domain, the value you enter in the Domain field must meet certain requirements.
            - [23bf0cb8c8dbae2690c6f88a2b9fdacbb5dffa10](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/23bf0cb8c8dbae2690c6f88a2b9fdacbb5dffa10.md) -- You can exclude domains to allow only basic-level logging on those domains.
          - [79efabc23316c487e98a98f540795f6661a2763a](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/79efabc23316c487e98a98f540795f6661a2763a.md) -- These properties allow you to control the behavior of outbound web service request logging.
        - [4b8282c1d52b8ed24cca2950fb26fbdd7316739e](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/4b8282c1d52b8ed24cca2950fb26fbdd7316739e.md) -- Mutual authentication establishes trust by exchanging Secure Sockets Layer (SSL) certificates.
          - [1bce3c8a16b234e986be529c43183ba09f1f0495](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/1bce3c8a16b234e986be529c43183ba09f1f0495.md) -- You can create a custom HTTPS protocol profile to specify the credentials and certificates used for outbound web services. For example, you can create a custom HTTPS protocol profile to enable mutual authentication.
          - [c15b85361a77819328797623d131685c785d9337](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/c15b85361a77819328797623d131685c785d9337.md) -- You can configure a SOAP or REST message for mutual authentication using a protocol profile.
      - [ed390b2caf9f2df362b05123d6e7eb02d85abe6c](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/ed390b2caf9f2df362b05123d6e7eb02d85abe6c.md) -- In addition to employing inbound and outbound web services to integrate with ServiceNow apps, other resources are available to you, such as scripted GraphQL, Javascript, and API developer guides.
        - [afe8f903ba4e7919079a60f02be87b29333f3eaa](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/afe8f903ba4e7919079a60f02be87b29333f3eaa.md) -- Use extension points to extend the functionality of an application without altering the original application code. You can use pre-existing extension points available in selected ServiceNow AI Platform applications, or you can add extension points when you develop custom applications in your own instance.
          - [4115cc5a6ab0efac52c694da73bc2bce1755928c](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/4115cc5a6ab0efac52c694da73bc2bce1755928c.md) -- Use the scripted extension points in the server-side script includes that store JavaScript functions and object classes. By using extension points, you can integrate customizations without actually altering the core components in the application code.
            - [1537f6940697de9a45dac400f6f7d170c06a6fa9](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/1537f6940697de9a45dac400f6f7d170c06a6fa9.md) -- When developing an application, create scripted extension points and add them to the script includes in the application code. Use a scripted extension point to designate the specific location where data or objects can be sent to a registered custom script include and where returned results are processed.
              - [680e3444e7ae55748b247431221bc304303aaa34](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/680e3444e7ae55748b247431221bc304303aaa34.md) -- Create a scripted extension point that can be placed in a script include in the base application code. By placing the scripted extension point, you designate the specific location in the application code where data or objects can be sent to a customization and where data is returned.
              - [b26c9133dfc088a34cd8116c80b304636f522c82](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/b26c9133dfc088a34cd8116c80b304636f522c82.md) -- Add the scripted extension point into a script include in the base application code. To add the extension point, include an API call that identifies the location at which registered custom artifacts execute.
          - [a7ae354f2abf2ccdb803a85be5be95328a28162e](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/a7ae354f2abf2ccdb803a85be5be95328a28162e.md) -- Use UI extension points in the server-side UI macros, such as HTML extensions, to add custom content to a UI page without having to directly modify the page. By using UI extension points, you can integrate customizations without actually altering the core components in the application code.
            - [2f18ec169455e1bd4640ecc9473cd081747c1f80](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/2f18ec169455e1bd4640ecc9473cd081747c1f80.md) -- When developing an application, create UI extension points and add them to the server-side UI macros, such as HTML extensions, or the UI pages in the base application code.
              - [74e78619373dab5073e226b4b93f12cb65236ad6](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/74e78619373dab5073e226b4b93f12cb65236ad6.md) -- Create a UI extension point that you can place in the UI macros, such as HTML extensions, in the application code. By placing the UI extension point, you designate the specific location in the application code where data or objects can be sent to a customization and where data is returned.
              - [e2d7c6ec1e997deb59d4e84bd2fb05df918b69c1](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/e2d7c6ec1e997deb59d4e84bd2fb05df918b69c1.md) -- Add the UI extension point into a UI macro or HTML extension in the base application code. To add the extension point, place an API call that identifies the location at which registered custom artifacts execute.
            - [5e5bffbb2827b1f6db78e9514baa5050280d1293](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/5e5bffbb2827b1f6db78e9514baa5050280d1293.md) -- Register custom UI macros against a selected UI extension point in the application code.
              - [d1a66ebb659f7c4abeea7df3f4de4ce54fdacaec](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/d1a66ebb659f7c4abeea7df3f4de4ce54fdacaec.md) -- Create a custom UI macro and code when you register it against a selected UI extension point.
              - [4e157e69a4ad2d4a9fb42a16b95934ae31eec3fd](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/4e157e69a4ad2d4a9fb42a16b95934ae31eec3fd.md) -- Review the listing of custom UI macros that are registered against a UI extension point. Extension instances indicate the application scope in which registered custom UI macros are used and provides a link to each. You can also activate or inactive an extension instance.
          - [5d4924ac8a565780ad9c50eb1ac7d07252d06c62](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/5d4924ac8a565780ad9c50eb1ac7d07252d06c62.md) -- Use client extension points in client-side UI scripting to modify forms, so that you do not have to directly modify the form. By using client extension points, you can integrate customizations without actually altering the core components in the application code.
            - [21960202c9bb33afbf17d452a73dcf5f98c2a361](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/21960202c9bb33afbf17d452a73dcf5f98c2a361.md) -- Create client extension points and add them to the client-side UI scripts in the base application code.
              - [233b6792b09ac24e146ad5a1d1607e4e1d361aad](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/233b6792b09ac24e146ad5a1d1607e4e1d361aad.md) -- Create a client extension point that you can place in the UI scripts in the application code. By placing the client extension point, you designate the specific location in the application code where data or objects can be sent to a customization, and where data is returned.
              - [32455331edb3c835a7ba465bac971f17de130bf1](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/32455331edb3c835a7ba465bac971f17de130bf1.md) -- Add the UI extension point into a UI script in the base application code. To add the extension point, place an API call that identifies the location at which registered custom artifacts execute.

Explore the API using the REST API Explorer to quickly construct and test REST requests for that table.
          - [cb0325c756f870dda0a5c67c9820fb03acaeb677](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/cb0325c756f870dda0a5c67c9820fb03acaeb677.md) -- Export a REST API as an OpenAPI specification to import it into another web services tool.

You can create rules to limit requests for specific users, users with specific roles, or all users.
          - [d45d8e452d9ea4306b9d9705a9c08081dcd200c4](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/d45d8e452d9ea4306b9d9705a9c08081dcd200c4.md) -- Create rate limit rules to limit the number of inbound REST API requests processed per hour.
          - [1464e8d967376d8cec9c68367c06e68259d06051](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/1464e8d967376d8cec9c68367c06e68259d06051.md) -- Reset a rate limit rule to reset the rate limit count to zero (0) and delete any violations for the current hour.
          - [c68f0b491f9d49b06556837726e0d28571a40ff0](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/c68f0b491f9d49b06556837726e0d28571a40ff0.md) -- To determine if you have set a rate limit rule appropriately, monitor the counts and violations for inbound REST API requests that are restricted by the rule.

        - [d5862b49bddb59faf7a4c6be35a1520259a4da37](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/d5862b49bddb59faf7a4c6be35a1520259a4da37.md) -- You can debug in-bound REST queries by reviewing the session debug log.
        - [3973cd5ae8211240ce9125dc81bc867e27ba21de](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/3973cd5ae8211240ce9125dc81bc867e27ba21de.md) -- You can include session debug logs in a REST response body by passing the X-WantSessionDebugMessages header in the request.
        - [0787694628f2aeb61ed81ce85bf2bf1244b7ef0d](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/0787694628f2aeb61ed81ce85bf2bf1244b7ef0d.md) -- When you define a cross-origin resource sharing (CORS) rule, the value you enter in the Domain field must meet certain requirements. Each CORS rule supports a single wildcard to match incoming Origin headers.

Setting the number of seconds to 0 will turn auto refresh off. By default, auto refresh is disabled. If intSeconds is greater than 0 and less than the minimum allowed time in seconds (10), it will be set to 10 seconds.

The format of the start and end dates must be in the default timestamp format: yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss. If the initialViewRange property is specified, it will override the default range.

It then uses the passed country and language codes to identify the locale of the number and returns the parsed number in that format.

The next status is based on the wf_activity.sys_id associated with the activity represented in the haRecord existing as a TO in a transition associated with any ActivityHistoryRecords that executed in the workflow's history. (This differs from getPreviousByTransition, which returns only TO transitions that come before the haRecord in the execution sequence (by time).) The return value is a collection of ActivityHistoryRecords that identify the argument haRecord.wfaId as their TO activity.

In the process of initializing the WorkflowModelManager object, getExecutedHistory() creates and populates the activityHistoryRecord data object array (which is a member of the WorkflowModelManager class). Most WorkflowModelManager methods return a subset of the activityHistoryRecord array. Your script can then call one or more of the activityHistoryRecord methods to accomplish further work.

On success, Assigned to field is empty and the value of the Rejected field is true for the specified work item.

        - [576a9dfd8e430c2f6027ec9eb443cac25ba1870b](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/576a9dfd8e430c2f6027ec9eb443cac25ba1870b.md) -- Determines the CMDB operation (insert/update) to perform with the specified payload (request body), without committing the operations in the database.

        - [761a260f59f4c974b118ee24ea89983fbbe7593a](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/761a260f59f4c974b118ee24ea89983fbbe7593a.md) -- Returns a specified user's granted and inherited roles.

        - [6df85eabe97cdc065b15647797bd6618dae0ea21](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/6df85eabe97cdc065b15647797bd6618dae0ea21.md) -- Returns the valid entitlement records based on the provided filter.
        - [46137f2bf978a0cf074ac72c2a9819443cd1709c](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/46137f2bf978a0cf074ac72c2a9819443cd1709c.md) -- Returns details of the specified entitlement characteristic record and its latest usage record.
      - [a1b95ac8f46bcbe1395a1198dddc4176e4b18cd9](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/a1b95ac8f46bcbe1395a1198dddc4176e4b18cd9.md) -- The Virtual Agent Bot Integration API provides endpoints that allow users to integrate any chat interface or a bot ecosystem with the ServiceNow Virtual Agent and/or Live Agent.
        - [17787c0534b8cd33ecf8582b40abbb9575b92ac5](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/17787c0534b8cd33ecf8582b40abbb9575b92ac5.md) -- Sends a specified message, attachment, or URL to a specified secondary ServiceNow Virtual Agent (VA) bot.
      - [b1d946e27e28881cbde62ad3b7890bda3b600981](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/b1d946e27e28881cbde62ad3b7890bda3b600981.md) -- The Voice Interaction API provides endpoints to transcribe and store voice interactions between agents and callers during a telephone conversation. It works in conjunction with Virtual Agent and Live Agent chats.
        - [2f34f3b942b3d7eec2c64dbfc6c4b64ecb25ccb8](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/2f34f3b942b3d7eec2c64dbfc6c4b64ecb25ccb8.md) -- Terminates the specified voice interaction. Once called, no additional voice messages are translated and stored for the phone conversation.
        - [badd0c599b71a5b1d75468b081b9ce620bb3dc1b](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/badd0c599b71a5b1d75468b081b9ce620bb3dc1b.md) -- Creates a voice interaction record within the Interaction [interaction] table.
        - [c4eb740256c4c54246b378b3138a0d2e90c267fb](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/c4eb740256c4c54246b378b3138a0d2e90c267fb.md) -- Creates a conversation record within the Conversation [sys_cs_conversation] table for the specified interaction.
        - [d975c8b04bc995a8c2f22d0f1cb66a724a55e3b7](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/d975c8b04bc995a8c2f22d0f1cb66a724a55e3b7.md) -- Adds the specified voice conversations messages to the specified voice transaction record. These messages are typically generated using third-party vendor software. Currently, only Amazon Connect is supported.
        - [78b8da4679d661039168188517dd2c1feaad0503](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/78b8da4679d661039168188517dd2c1feaad0503.md) -- Adds messages to an existing voice interaction after a call is completed. You can use this API to attach transcribed messages of a voice call between an agent and caller.
      - [d555e3cf60db2bbe1396ab5e7b6b8b0faa98d0b3](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/d555e3cf60db2bbe1396ab5e7b6b8b0faa98d0b3.md) -- The Workplace Connector Webhook API is a generic interface that enables data from different kinds of workplace hardware or sensors (such as badging systems or occupancy sensors) to be stored in Workplace Service Delivery tables.
        - [8eb8aa53c93c507205e0dd77c6d3879d6d73d900](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/8eb8aa53c93c507205e0dd77c6d3879d6d73d900.md) -- Creates a record in the Connector Events [sn_wsd_wc_connector_events] table and then stores the hardware/sensor-based event data passed in the endpoint's payload into the Payload field of that record.

        - [eb337ce28c5b8d48129ba83b065584cd37809a2c](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/eb337ce28c5b8d48129ba83b065584cd37809a2c.md) -- Before you can create mobile applications that interact with your ServiceNow instance, you must first download and install the ServiceNow Mobile SDK Android library.
        - [7336d6f1ff56321863c5278503ee09cb8ebd1ee9](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/7336d6f1ff56321863c5278503ee09cb8ebd1ee9.md) -- Before writing any application that leverages the Mobile SDK, you must install and configure the Mobile SDK scoped application.
        - [d58b575f8a5cee8809133a3ca0af350458a436c9](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/d58b575f8a5cee8809133a3ca0af350458a436c9.md) -- Before writing any application that leverages the Mobile SDK for Android, you must configure your project to use the SDK.
        - [f8fc7c901a36f30fdc7a7d1a9ca7b6a85b4bd693](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/f8fc7c901a36f30fdc7a7d1a9ca7b6a85b4bd693.md) -- You must configure your ServiceNow instance to accept and exchange a JSON Web Token (JWT) for an OAuth access token. To enable this functionality you must install a certificate that matches the JWT provider’s private key on the ServiceNow instance that you will use as the JWT Token consumer and create an OAuth Entity on that instance.
        - [c538029ce279f195c27f9a79e58ff9766b9b20c3](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/c538029ce279f195c27f9a79e58ff9766b9b20c3.md) -- To enable SDK services to be accessible by unauthenticated users, you must configure the Mobile SDK on your ServiceNow instance to allow guest user access. A guest user can view public web pages hosted in an instance using NowWeb, have a conversation as a guest using NowChat, access public data on the instance using NowTableService and NowGraphQLService, and access custom API services configured for unauthenticated users using NowAPIService.
      - [94021843bcd9b2d402b2bb7c10c226c9c8b7551b](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/94021843bcd9b2d402b2bb7c10c226c9c8b7551b.md) -- In order to use the Mobile SDK in your application, you need to perform SDK initialization, authorization, and logging.
      - [bf1253385022679aa40caa5bf09f4715ece61a31](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/bf1253385022679aa40caa5bf09f4715ece61a31.md) -- The Mobile SDK enables you to easily implement Virtual and Live Agent chat services within your Android application.
        - [d97c3810a428ce1154c251aaa2fef1033b3da241](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/d97c3810a428ce1154c251aaa2fef1033b3da241.md) -- You can pass an optional NowChatConfiguration parameter when starting a chat session to modify some of the behavior of NowChat.
      - [ea34d954764d0daf7ab1bd367972f6d221c35653](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/ea34d954764d0daf7ab1bd367972f6d221c35653.md) -- The Mobile SDK enables you to interact with data in tables that reside on your ServiceNow instance. You can interact with this data through the ServiceNow REST Table API using the NowTableService() API that directly calls the REST interface, or through the NowGraphQLService() API that executes a specified GraphQL query against the REST GraphQL API.
        - [a33bf7e9628d1150f2d59d482abfd8906aa39251](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/a33bf7e9628d1150f2d59d482abfd8906aa39251.md) -- Within the Mobile SDK, when interacting with data from a ServiceNow instance through a REST endpoint, you can configure what data is passed back in the return results.
        - [2bb53b31019ed44d3bcab4ce0a8c8042903494c0](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/2bb53b31019ed44d3bcab4ce0a8c8042903494c0.md) -- Within the Mobile SDK, when writing data to a ServiceNow instance through a REST endpoint, you can define field write options.
        - [9a8ff4c51bf4791be71139ebb46e6016b32cb36b](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/9a8ff4c51bf4791be71139ebb46e6016b32cb36b.md) -- Within the Mobile SDK, when interacting with data from a ServiceNow instance through a REST endpoint using the NowTableService, you can create a Paginator object that enables you to iterate over the records in the return results.
        - [f1350a76a3b06138813e2f75703bdbb62608e81e](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/f1350a76a3b06138813e2f75703bdbb62608e81e.md) -- If you want guest users to be able to interact with data within a table on your ServiceNow instance, you must configure the table to be guest accessible.
      - [2eb33db1e7ac75717bbff444cdfe811e661fc466](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/2eb33db1e7ac75717bbff444cdfe811e661fc466.md) -- The Mobile SDK provides functionality that enables Android applications to call the public REST API on a ServiceNow instance.
      - [02ab4da0df5f8e39c478c5c887073fd864a27353](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/02ab4da0df5f8e39c478c5c887073fd864a27353.md) -- The NowAttachmentService API enables you to perform CRUD operations on attachments and retrieve attachment metadata from your ServiceNow instance.
      - [8aeb2a79da70e16f4106a815cc4105da71bb5403](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/8aeb2a79da70e16f4106a815cc4105da71bb5403.md) -- You can display web pages hosted within your ServiceNow instance in your native Android application.
      - [e5f6d7ecbe0803ecc228e298bf8fb628ae0ebc2a](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/e5f6d7ecbe0803ecc228e298bf8fb628ae0ebc2a.md) -- Push notifications enable the sending of unsolicited messages (notifications) from a ServiceNow instance to a specific registered user and an application that leverages the ServiceNow Mobile SDK framework.
        - [6977faf12daf0b58926b600de908e790f47c4b04](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/6977faf12daf0b58926b600de908e790f47c4b04.md) -- For each Mobile SDK application that incorporates the Virtual Agent service and leverages push notifications, you must complete the following configuration process on your ServiceNow instance.

        - [200f1058642521bca998bab7847e2760a9886551](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/200f1058642521bca998bab7847e2760a9886551.md) -- In addition to the tasks that you must perform on your ServiceNow instance to configure a push notification, you must also include specific code in your Android application.

        - [874fa118dee92e9c82b3e5af2ae7a8cbf008f1a1](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/874fa118dee92e9c82b3e5af2ae7a8cbf008f1a1.md) -- Before writing any application that leverages the Mobile SDK for iOS, you must configure your project to use the SDK.

      - [d2adf4e1057c4c2d0e01322d18de4f14a4cb190f](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/d2adf4e1057c4c2d0e01322d18de4f14a4cb190f.md) -- To access the functionality provided by the NowSDK, you must first initialize the NowSDK in your application.

        - [64d5ccc2761164269b8f705c4124a18781bb45b2](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/64d5ccc2761164269b8f705c4124a18781bb45b2.md) -- For each Mobile SDK application that incorporates the Virtual Agent service and leverages push notifications, you must complete the following configuration process on your ServiceNow instance.

      - [c1994436b589f3e6dff64db05e1c1c0c00d04935](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/c1994436b589f3e6dff64db05e1c1c0c00d04935.md) -- The NowUIColoring interface contains all the colors used by all NowSDK modules.
    - [9a3229cb0d8c1abc19613b8e0f0a2428d188f2cb](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/9a3229cb0d8c1abc19613b8e0f0a2428d188f2cb.md) -- The Producer Event Notification Framework for Telecommunications, Media, and Technology enables the external distribution of events, such as trouble tickets, using a message-bus implemented in your network.
      - [4b31e6973e28cf918bfb84188bda167b41dd1d5f](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/4b31e6973e28cf918bfb84188bda167b41dd1d5f.md) -- If you are using the Hermes Messaging Service in your instance, you must configure the Producer Event Notification Framework for the Kafka message-bus.
      - [f899504f3a46f29a35d7b6eecac44824f146c167](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/f899504f3a46f29a35d7b6eecac44824f146c167.md) -- If you have deployed or plan to deploy the Open Message Bus in your instance, you must configure the Producer Event Notification Framework for this message-bus. You can deploy this message-bus for both cloud and on-premise instances.
      - [31f20a4ff1def771f3a8892c506ea38a16b7874d](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/31f20a4ff1def771f3a8892c506ea38a16b7874d.md) -- The base implementation of trouble ticket notification includes a set of trouble ticket events to demonstrate the capabilities of the system.
        - [ae3090db806dc4322a00e6b2f880fb11d2ae4b1b](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/ae3090db806dc4322a00e6b2f880fb11d2ae4b1b.md) -- When adding a new trouble ticket event to your implementation, you must add a business rule to your ServiceNow instance.
        - [adcb6e7aaa7af24c32afd6beb4138693aa63ab6a](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/adcb6e7aaa7af24c32afd6beb4138693aa63ab6a.md) -- When adding a trouble ticket event to your implementation, you must create a function that generates the required TMF-compliant payload for that event type. In addition, you must add that function to the case statement that directs the logic to this function when processing this type of event record.
        - [e723f140b9328a944fd0dfc00a4f72a751462e62](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/e723f140b9328a944fd0dfc00a4f72a751462e62.md) -- The base trouble ticket event notification implementation provides multiple default trouble ticket event type examples. You may need to alter the payloads for these events within your implementation to meet your actual needs. You may also need to configure the payload for any new trouble ticket events that you add to your implementation.
      - [626f5385a0c084226747bd59c696eca3bd6e3b40](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/626f5385a0c084226747bd59c696eca3bd6e3b40.md) -- The Event Management Topic Open API enables you to save topic records in the Topic [sn_api_notif_mgmt_topic] table after validating the topic payload.
    - [170538585f531e316c10b0d31a49412210f8dc38](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/170538585f531e316c10b0d31a49412210f8dc38.md) -- Use the Product Order Open API to create, update, and retrieve product order information.
      - [43a1f8196bdd04396351c97da35761a9656085e4](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/43a1f8196bdd04396351c97da35761a9656085e4.md) -- The Product Order Open API can be extended by editing script includes.
    - [56f0ba9852d6be5c0fd3dbb91ea1c5adb082ac53](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/56f0ba9852d6be5c0fd3dbb91ea1c5adb082ac53.md) -- Use the Resource Inventory Open API to create, retrieve, update, and delete resources such as interface cards, logical and physical connections, network sites, and more.
      - [709a0a261355b017108cccad5ab47f86c5bff7ea](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/709a0a261355b017108cccad5ab47f86c5bff7ea.md) -- The Resource Inventory Open API can be extended using extension points and script includes.
    - [a4673fdd202ade759b2633ac6858ec51d795563d](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/a4673fdd202ade759b2633ac6858ec51d795563d.md) -- Scoped caches allow customers to store custom data in memory for faster access, and define when it becomes invalidated. This can be useful when working with data takes longer to obtainthat from scratch, such as the results of a query across multiple tables. Use the ScopedCacheManager API to retrieve, set, and flush cache data for scoped applications.
      - [70edecac7e845c26af8d3c683d1f4ba59e2f3635](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/xanadu/markdown/employee-service-management/70edecac7e845c26af8d3c683d1f4ba59e2f3635.md) -- Create a scoped cache and define its cache pair.
